summary: DHH expresses unbridled enthusiasm for the newly unveiled iMac at MacWorld, calling it the most exciting computer he's ever seen. He watched Steve Jobs' keynote twice and declares his existing PCs obsolete by comparison. He also praises OS X and Apple's suite of iLife apps. The post ends with his determination to save enough money to buy the iMac when it launches in Denmark in March 2002.
key_points: The 2002 iMac redesign (the iconic lamp-style model) represented a leap beyond expectations of tablets or PDAs
DHH was already an Apple convert and OS X enthusiast very early in the platform's life
The post captures a moment when Apple's design philosophy was winning over technical users who had been on PCs
money_quotes: It wasn't a webtablet or a PDA, it was so much more.
It's so beautiful, so clean, so right.
Now where can I find some poor smock who still haven't seen the iMac and make him buy my two tangled desktop PC's?
key_insight: Even in 2002, DHH's instinct for design quality and willingness to abandon the status quo for something better was already fully formed—foreshadowing the aesthetic sensibility he would later bring to Rails and Basecamp.
